content:
Character: Orion Hale
Perception: Orion is thin and pale, dark circles under dark eyes, beautiful in a bruised, fragile way. He comes into the coffee shop and sits in the corner like he's trying to disappear into the wall. I started leaving herbal tea at his table — chamomile, lemon balm — and he never said thank you but he always drank it. One day he looked up and said, "This one's better than last week's." That's our whole friendship. I think he's carrying something enormous. I don't know what. I see him as someone who needs gentleness without being asked if he's okay. I hope he sees me as safe.
summary:
Character: Orion Hale
Perception: Orion is thin and pale, dark circles under dark eyes, beautiful in a bruised, fragile way. He comes into the coffee shop and sits in the corner like he's trying to disappear into the wall. I started leaving herbal tea at his table — chamomile, lemon balm — and he never said thank you but he always drank it. One day he looked up and said, "This one's better than last week's." That's our whole friendship. I think he's carrying something enormous. I don't know what. I see him as someone who needs gentleness without being asked if he's okay. I hope he sees me as safe.
